name: Basílica de Santiago de Lokiz y Ojos de Ollobarren – circular desde Ganuza
region_intro: Hiking around Metauten offers exploration of a landscape characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and notable geological features such as rocky outcrops and natural springs. The region's terrain provides a mix of ascents and descents, often leading through rural settings and past historical sites. These trails are predominantly moderate in difficulty, making them accessible for regular hikers. The area is part of the Lokiz mountain range, contributing to its varied topography. Best hiking trails around Metauten The most popular hiking route is Basílica de Santiago de Lokiz y Ojos de Ollobarren – circular desde Ganuza, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route visits a historic basilica and natural springs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Saint James of Lokiz Basilica and Ollobarren Eye Trail, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This trail explores the area around the basilica and a significant natural spring. Local hikers also love the Santiago Rock – Ojo de Ollobarren Cave loop from Ganuza, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through rocky terrain and past a cave entrance, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Metauten is defined by forested hills, rocky formations, and natural water features, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Metauten are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Metauten's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Metauten?
A: Metauten offers a selection of 11 hiking routes. The majority of these, 10 to be exact, are considered moderate in difficulty, with one trail rated as difficult, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Metauten?
A: The hiking trails in Metauten are predominantly moderate, making them accessible for regular hikers. There is also one difficult route for those seeking a greater challenge. No easy trails are listed in the current selection.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Metauten?
A: The trails in Metauten are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied topography, which includes forested hills, rocky formations, and natural water features.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Metauten?
A: Yes, Metauten features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970319179/basilica-de-santiago-de-lokiz-y-ojos-de-ollobarren-circular-desde-ganuza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Santiago de Lokiz y Ojos de Ollobarren – circular desde Ganuza&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.3 km) long and visits a historic basilica and natural springs. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Rock – Ojo de Ollobarren Cave loop from Ganuza&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.7-mile (9.2 km) trail through rocky terrain.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or longer hikes in Metauten?
A: For those looking for a longer and more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10515699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de Ollobarren Cave – OjoDeSanPrudencio loop from Ganuza&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice. This route spans 8.1 miles (13.1 km) and involves a significant elevation gain, offering a more demanding hike through the region's diverse landscape.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on Metauten's trails?
A: Hiking in Metauten allows you to explore a landscape rich in natural and historical features. You'll encounter rolling hills, forested areas, and notable geological formations like rocky outcrops and natural springs. Key attractions include the Basílica de Santiago de Lokiz and the Ojos de Ollobarren. You can also find nearby mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6931774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road and Portillo de Piedramillera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any good viewpoints along the hiking trails in Metauten?
A: Yes, the region's varied topography, part of the Lokiz mountain range, offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find excellent vistas from locations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3711218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Piedramillera&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a mountain pass, providing panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Metauten?
A: The best time to hike in Metauten is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter hiking is possible, but some trails might be more challenging due to weather conditions.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Metauten?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, most rural hiking areas in Spain, including those around Metauten, typically offer designated parking spaces or roadside parking near popular starting points, especially in villages like Ganuza, which serves as a common access point for several routes.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails in Metauten by public transport?
A: Access to the more remote trailheads in Metauten by public transport can be limited. While local bus services might connect larger towns in the region, reaching specific starting points for hikes often requires a car. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for the specific village you plan to start from.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Metauten?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in rural areas like Metauten, provided they are kept under control, especially around livestock or wildlife. It's always a good practice to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers and to protect the local environment.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking trails in Metauten?
A: While the trails are predominantly moderate, some shorter circular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970319179/basilica-de-santiago-de-lokiz-y-ojos-de-ollobarren-circular-desde-ganuza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Santiago de Lokiz y Ojos de Ollobarren – circular desde Ganuza&lt;/a&gt; (3.9 miles), can be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to regular walking. These routes often combine natural beauty with points of interest, keeping younger hikers engaged.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking routes in Metauten?
A: Metauten is a rural area, so while there might not be cafes directly on every trailhead, you can typically find small local establishments, such as bars or restaurants, in the nearby villages like Ganuza. It's always a good idea to pack snacks and water, especially for longer hikes, and check village opening hours in advance.
